tool: mlx.metal.start_capture() → .gputrace bundle + binary inspection of embedded MetalLib (strings + disassembly fingerprinting)
tool_notes: Earlier characterisation of Track K as 'needs Xcode Instruments' was wrong. `mlx.metal.start_capture(path)` produces a .gputrace bundle from Python without Xcode being installed. The bundle includes a MetalLib executable from which we can extract every kernel name, its disassembly, and per-bit-width code-path differences. Full kernel timing still benefits from Instruments, but the structural root cause is observable here.

workload.model: qwen3_dense / Qwen3.5-9B-MLX-4bit with model.rotated.safetensors (64 ffn_gate/ffn_up re-quantised to 3-bit affine after Hadamard rotation)

root_cause_finding.summary: MLX's affine_qmv_{fast,quad} kernels at bits=3 (and bits=5) call a helper function `qdot<...>` per element, while the bits=2/4/6/8 variants inline the dequantisation directly. The qdot call adds per-element overhead that dominates when the model spends most decode wall time in qmv (which Qwen3.5-9B 4096-dim FFN does).
evidence_from_disassembly: affine_qmv_fast_bfloat16_t_gs_64_b_4 does NOT call qdot; uses inline llvm.fmuladd.f32 + air.simd_sum.f32
affine_qmv_fast_bfloat16_t_gs_64_b_5 also calls qdot<float, 16, 5> — bits=5 has same handicap
Pattern: bits ∈ {3, 5} ⇒ qdot call; bits ∈ {2, 4, 6, 8} ⇒ inline. Likely because 32 / bits is non-integer for 3 and 5, forcing a non-aligned u32 unpacking helper.
root_cause_finding.hypothesis: A 3-bit qmv kernel that handles the non-aligned packing inline (e.g. via 96-bit-aligned reads — three u32 words = 32 3-bit values) should match or beat the 4-bit kernel on per-byte throughput. The kernel author opportunity is precisely this re-pack-aware inline path.

intervention_options.scope: Patch the qdot helper or the qmv_impl<bits=3> path to inline the 96-bit-aligned unpacking. Most efficient long-term; benefits every MLX consumer.
intervention_options.estimated_effort: 1-2 weeks Metal C++ + MLX PR cycle

intervention_options.scope: Compile a custom `ax_qmv_b3_gs64_bfloat16` Metal kernel in `metal/`, register it as a Track-K kernel under ADR 0020, route 3-bit ffn_gate/ffn_up matmuls to it.
Accept that 3-bit is structurally slower on MLX and switch the calibration spike to improving 4-bit re-quant quality (currently 0.73 ratio). No kernel work needed.

decision_gate_status: Gate from previous artifact resolved: the 3-bit qmv kernel is NOT bandwidth-bound — it is dispatch/compute-bound on the qdot helper call. Per the decision gate, this means '3-bit as bandwidth-win is the wrong attack' UNLESS the kernel itself is rewritten. The two viable forward paths are (a) kernel rewrite or (b) abandon 3-bit and improve 4-bit calibration. Choosing one is a planning decision, not an evidence decision.
